steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The passage describes an action (NASA crashed a probe into Dimorphos) immediately followed by its result (the moonlet's orbital period was permanently altered). The transition word must signal this causal flow.

steps.title: Match the causal signal
steps.content: ”Consequently” directly expresses as a result and shows that the orbital period change is the outcome of the probe impact. The other three choices invoke different logical relationships — comparison, addition, and specification — none of which fit a cause-and-effect sequence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
